page: Microsoft Azure E48ps_v6 is an arm64-based virtual machine belonging to the Epsv6 server family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 48 dedicated physical cores powered by the Microsoft ARM Neoverse-N2 processor operating at 3.4 GHz. The CPU configuration includes a 128 MB L3 cache, 1.0 MB L2 cache per core, and 64 KB L1d cache. This instance is equipped with 384.0 GB of RAM, establishing an 8.0 GB memory-to-core ratio. It does not include local storage or bundled GPUs, but it is Premium Storage capable.

In benchmark evaluations, the E48ps_v6 demonstrates top-tier memory performance. Large-block read-only memory bandwidth reaches 463.87 GB/sec, and write bandwidth registers at 200.05 GB/sec, both placing in the top 10% of tested systems. This high memory throughput is reflected in a top-tier PassMark database operations score of 37,055.11 and a Geekbench multi-core score of 21,904.0. Multi-core CPU performance via stress-ng is strong at 99,340.45, while single-core performance across stress-ng and Geekbench remains in the average tier.

Given its high memory bandwidth and 384.0 GB RAM capacity, this server is configured for memory-intensive workloads such as database operations, caching layers, and multi-threaded data processing. Redis SET operations perform strongly at 89.22 million operations per second. However, the lack of local storage requires external storage attachment, and the absence of AVX or AMX instruction sets limits its suitability for specialized vector math or heavy local machine learning workloads, as shown by weak prompt processing speeds on large 70B LLM models.
description: Microsoft Azure E48ps_v6 is an arm64 virtual machine from the Epsv6 family, powered by 48 dedicated physical cores of a 3.4 GHz Microsoft ARM Neoverse-N2 processor. It features 384.0 GB of RAM, yielding an 8.0 GB memory-to-core ratio, but contains no local storage or GPUs. Operating under Microsoft Hyper-V, this instance delivers top-tier memory bandwidth, with large-block read speeds of 463.87 GB/sec and write speeds of 200.05 GB/sec. Benchmark results highlight strong multi-core processing, including a Geekbench multi-core score of 21,904.0 and 89.22 million Redis SET operations per second. Conversely, single-core performance and large LLM prompt processing remain average to weak. Lacking local storage and AVX/AMX vector extensions, this server is optimized for memory-intensive workloads, database operations, and high-throughput caching systems rather than raw vector mathematics or local storage-heavy tasks.
og_description: Microsoft Azure E48ps_v6 is an arm64 virtual machine featuring 48 dedicated ARM Neoverse-N2 cores, 384 GB of RAM, and top-tier memory bandwidth, optimized for database and memory-intensive workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure E48ps_v6 is an arm64 VM with 48 dedicated Neoverse-N2 cores and 384 GB RAM, delivering top-tier memory bandwidth for databases.
tagline: An ARM-based memory-optimized virtual machine featuring forty-eight dedicated cores and high-bandwidth memory for demanding database and caching workloads.
bullet_points: 48 dedicated Microsoft ARM Neoverse-N2 physical cores running at 3.4 GHz
384.0 GB of RAM providing an 8.0 GB memory-to-core ratio
Top-tier memory read bandwidth of 463.87 GB/sec and write bandwidth of 200.05 GB/sec
No bundled local storage or GPUs, requiring external storage attachment
Strong multi-core performance with 89.22 million Redis SET operations per second
Suited for high-throughput database operations, caching, and memory-intensive applications
